title: Claude Config Explorer
subtitle: Your Claude Code setup at a glance - plugins, skills, subagents, MCP servers, hooks, and settings.

hookScripts.title: ~/.claude/hooks/ scripts
hookScripts.subtitle: Helper scripts referenced by your hook commands. These run when settings.json `hooks.<event>.hooks[].command` invokes them.

memory.autoSubtitle: Per-project file-based memory under ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/ — a MEMORY.md index plus one file per remembered fact. Read-only here.
memory.noMatches: No memory files match your search.
memory.nameHelp: Filename ending in .md (.md is added if you omit it).
memory.projectHelp: Created in this project's memory dir.

edit.nameHelp: Letters, digits, dot, dash, underscore. Max 64 chars.
edit.confirmDeleteBody: This will move the file (or skill folder, including bundled assets) to a timestamped backup before removing it. The backup lives outside the directories Claude Code scans, so the entry will disappear from your CLI.

templates.skills:
---
name: my-skill
description: One-line description (shown when Claude picks the skill)
---

Body - what the skill does, when to use it, examples.
templates.agents:
---
name: my-agent
description: When to invoke this subagent
tools: Read, Grep
model: sonnet
---

System prompt for the subagent.
templates.commands:
---
description: One-line description shown in the slash menu
---

What /my-command does.
templates.output-styles:
---
name: my-style
description: Output style description
---

Style instructions.
templates.memory:
# Project memory

Claude reads this on session start.
templates.auto-memory:
---
name: my-fact
description: One-line summary of the remembered fact
metadata:
  type: feedback
---

The fact to remember.

backups.title: Backups
backups.empty: No backups yet - every edit or delete creates a timestamped backup that lands here.
backups.subtitle: Every save / delete in the editor writes a copy here first. Backup folders live outside the directories Claude Code scans, so they don't reappear as ghosts in your CLI.
backups.restoreHint: Restore by renaming the backup back into place:

hooks.title: Hooks are read-only here on purpose
hooks.body: Hooks live in the `hooks` block of `settings.json` files that Claude Code writes to constantly. Editing them from the dashboard would race with the live CLI and could silently break the dashboard's own ingestion (the `hook-handler.js` entry below).
hooks.howTo: Edit them yourself with one of:
hooks.cmd1Note: Interactive hook editor inside Claude Code

settings.title: Settings are read-only here on purpose
settings.body: These JSON files are rewritten by the live `claude` CLI on every model change, theme toggle, plugin enable, etc. A dashboard write would race with the CLI and lose state.
settings.howTo: Edit settings via Claude Code itself:
settings.cmd2Note: Open the interactive config UI inside the CLI
settings.cmd3Note: Hand-edit (only when no `claude` process is running)

plugins.title: Plugins are managed by the CLI
plugins.body: Plugins are bundles with their own registry - `installed_plugins.json` plus install paths under `plugins/cache/`. The dashboard reads them but defers install/uninstall to `claude plugin …` so the registry stays consistent.

mcp.title: MCP servers are managed by the CLI
mcp.body: MCP server config lives inside `~/.claude.json`, the same file the live CLI writes to constantly. Concurrent writes from the dashboard would corrupt project state, so add/remove flow goes through Claude Code itself.
